items.title: Klatham 'waiting in the wings'
items.desc: In Thailand's fluid and transactional political landscape, the emergence of the Klatham Party as a "waiting wing" opposition reflects a calibrated strategy.

items.title: AI race spurs digital self-reliance push
items.desc: The issue of sovereign artificial intelligence (AI) is emerging as a strategic priority for governments and enterprises worldwide, as countries seek greater control over critical digital infrastructure, data and computing capabilities.

items.title: Plastic packaging at risk as war hits crucial raw material supplies
items.desc: Packaging may be the next industry to bear the brunt of petrochemical supply disruptions if the Israel-US war on Iran does not abate, says Veera Kwanloetchit, a director of the Public Private Partnership for Sustainable Plastic and Waste Management.

items.title: Booksellers predict upbeat market
items.desc: The Thai book industry predicts a resilient year despite ongoing conflicts in the Middle East, according to the Publishers and Booksellers Association of Thailand (Pubat).

items.title: Energy security now a daunting problem
items.desc: Amid spikes in global crude oil and gas prices, the government has deployed several measures including price caps to ensure energy security and ease the cost of living.

items.title: THAI forced to increase ticket prices
items.desc: The war in the Middle East has driven up operating cost for Thai Airways International (THAI), with jet fuel prices rising by more than twofold, forcing the national carrier to increase average ticket prices by 10-15% to stay afloat.

items.title: Traders, shoppers sound price warnings
items.desc: Rising fuel costs linked to the Middle East conflict are driving up consumer prices, with traders warning of mounting pressure on both businesses and households.

items.title: Fuel panic spreads as pumps run dry
items.desc: Long queues at petrol stations across Thailand and "out of fuel" signs for diesel, the backbone of the transport sector, have raised public concern over the past week about the country's ability to manage energy reserves.

items.title: Thailand Post trumpets EV  fleet as fuel costs mount
items.desc: State-run logistics provider Thailand Post is emphasising replacement of petrol-powered vehicles with electric vehicles (EVs) for its last-mile delivery fleet to deal with volatile fuel prices.

items.title: Govt to tackle energy as 1st priority
items.desc: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul is expected to submit his cabinet list for royal endorsement today or tomorrow, with his administration due to take office next week.

items.title: Ministry launches price-fixing blitz
items.desc: The Internal Trade Department has uncovered multiple violations after checking fuel stations and markets, warning that unjustified hikes will face legal action.

items.title: Ministry eyes oil excise  tax trims
items.desc: The Finance Ministry is weighing the extent of potential oil tax cuts to keep retail fuel prices at a level the economy can absorb, while also avoiding excessive impact on government revenue.

items.title: Valuing electronic waste
items.desc: The trio of Advanced Info Service (AIS), Central Group and Japan Airlines have strengthened their collaboration on environmental, social and governance (ESG) missions to ensure an improved and sustainable future.

items.title: Sukhothai welcomes 'Sukh Jai Craft 2026'
items.desc: Sukhothai is set to welcome visitors to "Sukh Jai Craft 2026," a creative festival celebrating craftsmanship, culture, and community. Taking place from Wednesday until Sunday, between 5pm and 10pm at Prem Suk Water Park in Mueang Sukhothai, the event invites audiences to experience the province through the theme "Craft of Happiness: A City Designed for Joy."

items.title: The making of a mind
items.desc: Prime Video revisits one of literature's most enduring characters with Young Sherlock, a new drama that explores the early life of the legendary detective long before he becomes the calculating figure audiences know from Baker Street. Created by Matthew Parkhill and directed by Guy Ritchie, the eight-episode series is now available for streaming on Prime Video.

items.title: Tech, services expos run jointly at Impact
items.desc: More than 400 domestic and international companies will present cutting-edge technologies and services at ASEE 2026 and ARHC 2026 at Impact Exhibition Centre, Muang Thong Thani, from Wednesday to Friday.

items.title: SCB 10X launches AI YouTube series
items.desc: SCB 10X has launched "AI-Volution The Series 2026", a year-long online content series for executives, entrepreneurs and anyone interested in the future of AI.

items.title: Two wheels over baht buses as Pattaya visitors weigh freedom against safety
items.desc: A foreign visitor rides a motorbike with a Thai partner through Pattaya traffic, reflecting a common transport choice among tourists seeking flexibility, even as rising fuel concerns and busy roads raise safety questions, while others still prefer walking or hopping on baht buses to get around the city. (Photo by Jetsada Homklin) PATTAYA, Thailand – For many visitors exploring Pattaya, renting or even buying a motorbike has become one of the easiest ways to get around the city. With transport costs, fuel prices, and convenience all part of the daily calculation, two wheels often win over publi

items.title: Rider critically hurt after losing control and crashing into tree in South Pattaya
items.desc: The rider, identified as a 45-year-old Thai man, lies seriously injured on the sidewalk after reportedly losing control of his motorbike and crashing into a roadside tree on Sukhumvit Road in South Pattaya. PATTAYA, Thailand – A 45-year-old motorcyclist was seriously injured after losing control of his motorbike and crashing into a tree on Sukhumvit Road in the early hours of March 22. Rescue workers from the Sawang Boriboon responded to the accident at around 4:16 a.m. near the South Pattaya traffic light intersection. At the scene, rescuers found a heavily damaged Honda Scoopy‑i motorcycle o

items.title: Classical music evening at Ben’s Theater Jomtien on March 26
items.desc: The performance will feature acclaimed musicians Eri Nakagawa on piano and Omiros Yavroumis on violin. PATTAYA, Thailand – Classical music lovers in Pattaya will have a special opportunity to enjoy an evening of chamber music when Ben’s Theater Jomtien presents a live concert at 8 p.m. on Thursday, March 26, 2026. The performance will feature acclaimed musicians Eri Nakagawa on piano and Omiros Yavroumis on violin, both associated with the Mahidol University College of Music. The evening’s programme will include works by Robert Schumann, César Franck and other composers from the cl

items.title: Railway parallel road project in Pattaya delayed after rain damages groundwork
items.desc: Workers continue construction along the railway parallel road between Khao Talo and Wat Tham intersection, where rain-soaked ground has delayed final paving and forced additional soil testing. PATTAYA, Thailand – Construction of the southern railway parallel road between Khao Talo and the Wat Tham intersection has been delayed after recent rain affected the road base, officials reported on March 22. The project had originally been scheduled for completion by March 17, but heavy rainfall caused water to accumulate on the construction site. The moisture affected the compacted soil layers, with s

items.title: Thailand calls on public to switch off lights for global ‘Earth Hour’ on March 28
items.desc: Ms. Lalida Perviwatthana, Deputy Government Spokesperson, invites the public to join the global Earth Hour campaign by switching off lights for one hour on March 28. PATTAYA, Thailand – The Thai government is inviting the public to take part in Earth Hour 2026, a global campaign encouraging people to switch off lights for one hour to raise awareness about climate change. Ms. Lalida Perviwatthana, Deputy Government Spokesperson, announced that the international environmental initiative will take place on Saturday, March 28, 2026, from 8:30 PM to 9:30 PM, when individuals, businesses, and organi

items.title: Romantic retreats at The Landmark Bangkok
items.desc: Escape into a world of romance and refined luxury with Romantic Retreats, an exclusive stay package at The Landmark Bangkok, designed for unforgettable moments in the heart of the city. An Indulgent Escape for Two Escape into a world of romance and refined luxury with Romantic Retreats , an exclusive stay package at The Landmark Bangkok, thoughtfully designed for couples seeking an unforgettable getaway. Blending exceptional dining, holistic wellness, and elegant accommodation, this special package offers a perfect balance of indulgence and relaxation in the heart of the city. Rib Room & B

items.title: Warng Warng Taiwanese Shabu: just 30 minutes drive from Pattaya
items.desc: Warng Warng Original Taiwanese Shabu near Phala Beach in Ban Chang, a family-owned eatery offering a refreshing alternative to the busy Pattaya and Jomtien dining scene. BANCHANG, Thailand – Warng Warng Original Taiwanese Shabu is a family-owned restaurant near Phala Beach, Ban Chang, a refreshing alternative to the familiar Pattaya and Jomtien scene. Shabu was originally a Japanese hotpot of thinly sliced meat and stirred vegetables boiled in water and served with various dipping sauces. But the Taiwanese version uses less oil and is generally regarded as healthier as well as tastier. (

items.title: Finnish pedestrian killed after big bike collision on Jomtien Second Road
items.desc: A heavily damaged Kawasaki Z800 big bike was found overturned in the road after the rider struck a pedestrian and skidded into a parked car, leaving a 55-year-old Finnish man who had been crossing the road without a pulse; he later died despite rescue workers’ CPR attempts before being rushed to hospital. PATTAYA, Thailand – A foreign pedestrian was killed after being struck by a high-powered motorcycle on Jomtien Second Road late Saturday night (March 21), prompting an investigation by Pattaya City Police. Police said the accident occurred around 9:40 p.m. near the Rompho Market area. Emergen

items.title: Heat and isolated thunderstorms expected in Pattaya this week
items.desc: Locals and foreign visitors ride motorbikes through Pattaya’s heat as temperatures climb above 35°C, with bottled drinking water vendors seeing brisk business along busy streets. (Photo by Jetsada Homklin) PATTAYA, Thailand – Residents and visitors in Pattaya can expect hot daytime temperatures with the possibility of scattered thunderstorms and gusty winds, according to the latest forecast from the Thai Meteorological Department . Weather officials say upper Thailand remains under the influence of a heat-induced low-pressure system, bringing warm conditions during the day while southerly and

items.title: Oil panic could ruin Songkran as survey shows public anxiety and weak confidence in government
items.desc: Songkran revelers celebrate along Pattaya’s streets during last year’s festivities. A new nationwide poll suggests fuel concerns could affect travel plans for this year’s holiday. (Photo by Jetsada Homklin) PATTAYA, Thailand – With the Songkran holiday approaching, a new nationwide survey suggests that concerns over a possible oil shortage and declining confidence in the government could dampen travel plans during what is normally the country’s busiest festive period. The survey, conducted by the National Institute of Development Administration through its NIDA Poll research center, gathered r

items.title: Fake building permit racket uncovered on Koh Samui
items.desc: SURAT THANI - Authorities on Koh Samui are widening an investigation into construction permit forgery involving at least 10 projects and potentially involving both municipal staff and outsiders.

items.title: Travel warning: meningococcal disease reported in UK
items.desc: The Department of Disease Control has warned Thais travelling to the UK that an outbreak of meningococcal disease, a rare and severe bacterial infection, has been reported in students at schools and universities in England.

items.title: Diesel shortages hit provinces, pumps dry
items.desc: Fuel shortages, particularly diesel, have intensified across several provinces, with long queues forming at petrol stations, supplies running out within hours and motorists left waiting without certainty of refuelling.

items.title: SAO details structural failures that caused tower's collapse
items.desc: A year after its new headquarters tower crumpled during an earthquake, the State Audit Office (SAO) has outlined four critical structural and construction failures that led to the collapse.

items.title: Musk plans Tesla, SpaceX, xAI chip making lab in Texas
items.desc: NEW YORK  -- Elon Musk says his Terafab project - a grand plan to eventually manufacture his own chips for robotics, artificial intelligence and space data centres - will be built in Austin and jointly run by Tesla and SpaceX.

items.title: Filipino woman who just gave birth arrested after leaving newborn in black bag at Bangkok shopping mall
items.desc: BANGKOK — A 25-year-old Filipino woman has been arrested after the body of a newborn baby was found inside a black plastic bag in a women’s restroom at a major shopping mall on Sukhumvit Road, Bangkok. The incident came to light at 16:24 on 20 March when cleaning staff discovered the bag while performing routine trash collection in a storage room next to the women’s restroom. According to witnesses, the bag felt unusually heavy. Security personnel were immediately notified, and police, forensic teams, and medical examiners were called to investigate. Initial examination found the baby, a male,

items.title: Trump’s mixed messages on Iran: ‘Winding down’ the war and easing sanctions but adding more troops
items.desc: President Donald Trump frequently contradicts himself, sometimes in the same speech, social media post or even sentence. Within the space of a few hours Friday, he sent a torrent of mixed signals about the Iran war that raise more questions about the direction of the conflict and his administration’s strategy . During this time, Trump said he was considering winding down the war, his administration confirmed it was sending more troops to the Middle East and, in an effort to lessen the economic impact on global energy markets, the United States lifted sanctions on some Iranian oil for the

items.title: Monitor lizards flourish in central Bangkok park
items.desc: Nicknamed the "Bangkok Dragon", the Asian water monitor has become an unexpected tourist draw thanks to its unusually calm, non-aggressive nature.  These lizards are widespread beyond Lumpini Park, thriving along canals and wetlands across the capital and even appearing near Government House — where, some quip, honest politicians diligently work for the people.

items.title: Trump threatens attacks on Iranian power plants if Tehran fails to open the Strait of Hormuz
items.desc: CAIRO (AP) — Iran responded Sunday with threats of its own, a day after President Donald Trump warned the United States will “obliterate” Iran’s power plants if Tehran fails to fully open the Strait of Hormuz in 48 hours and Iranian missiles struck two cities near Israel’s main nuclear research center, injuring dozens and shattering apartment buildings. The developments signaled the war in the Middle East, now in its fourth week , was moving in a dangerous new direction. Sirens blared across Israel as Iran launched new barrages Sunday. In the country’s south, residents faced

items.title: 首次曝光！泰国被罢黜王子公开长女
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月21日，泰国瓦查勒颂王子在其个人社交媒体账号上发布图文，首次公开其长女照片，并为女儿送上生日祝福。这也是他首次通过社交媒体向外界披露女儿的形象。 照片中，瓦查勒颂王子与长女外出用餐庆祝生日，桌上摆放着点燃蜡烛的蛋糕。另有两张照片则展现了女儿作为运动员专注投入的一面。据悉，她即将参加国家级比赛。 瓦查勒颂王子在配文中写道：“最近是我大女儿的生日。她是一名获得认可、拥有排名的运动员。她所在的团队将在本周末参加国家级比赛。祝你好运，我的宝贝。” 关于感情经历，据泰媒报道，现年44岁的瓦查勒颂王子曾与美籍意大利裔女子伊丽莎·玛丽·加拉法诺结婚，育有两名女儿。瓦查勒颂王子本人表示，两人已离婚多年，但仍共同承担抚养子女的责任。不过也有传闻称，这段关系尚未完全了结，甚至至今仍涉及法律纠纷。 此外，他还曾与居住在纽约的乌克兰模特兼摄影师阿纳斯塔西娅·尼基希娜传出绯闻，但当事人始终未作回应，相关消息此后逐渐平息。 近期，瓦查勒颂王子与泰匈混血女性普拉达·坦西塔·迪拉功南公开恋情。她现年28岁，曾获2024年泰国环球小姐季军及2024年环球小姐亚军。两人多次一同外出约会、旅行，并曾在社交媒体上进行直播，被外界视为一种“非正式官宣”。 据了解，瓦查勒颂王子为泰国十世王陛下与第二任妻子所生，1996年被废黜王室身份后，与同母兄弟长期流亡海外，近三十年间极少有消息传出。2023年8月，

items.title: Government says Thailand records three meningococcal deaths
items.desc: Government says Thailand records three meningococcal deaths Legacy The Thai government said today, March 22, that it has instructed the Department of Disease Control (DDC) to closely monitor an outbreak of meningococcal disease in the United Kingdom, with three deaths reported in Thailand . Deputy government spokesperson Airin Phanrit said information from international health bodies, including the UK Health Security Agency and the European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control, showed the outbreak had been concentrated in southern parts of the UK and largely involved students. As of March

items.title: Poll: Doubts over oil reserves, Songkran travel at risk
items.desc: A sizeable share of Thais lack confidence that the country’s oil reserves will last the promised 98 days or that the government can secure additional supplies, and some will cancel Songkran travel plans if the crunch persists, according to a survey by the National Institute of Development Administration (Nida).

items.title: Police called after naked man walks into Khon Kaen petrol station
items.desc: Police called after naked man walks into Khon Kaen petrol station Legacy Police in Khon Kaen took a man into custody and sent him to the hospital today, March 22, after staff at a petrol station reported a naked man inside the premises, with rescuers saying he may have been experiencing a mental health episode . The incident was reported at about 11.30am at a PT station in Nong Kung Thanasan subdistrict, Phu Wiang district. Police from Phu Wiang Police Station and rescue workers responded after being alerted by station employees. Photo via Amarin TV Rescue chief Kitti said responders found the

items.title: Apple slashes China App Store fees to 25% following regulatory talks
items.desc: Apple has announced a reduction in App Store fees for developers in China, following discussions with Chinese regulators, in a move seen as easing regulatory pressure in one of its key markets.

items.title: Man alleges gunpoint abduction and assault linked to ex’s family
items.desc: Man alleges gunpoint abduction and assault linked to ex’s family Legacy A man in Nonthaburi filed a police complaint on March 20 after he was allegedly taken from outside his home by three men linked to his ex-girlfriend’s family in a pickup truck and later assaulted in the early hours of March 19 The man, identified as 31 year old Nawi said he reported the incident after seeking medical treatment for serious injuries. He had reportedly suffered injuries to his face and head, a broken nose, swelling to one ear, and a dislocated left shoulder, along with marks to his neck. Photo via Amarin TV A

items.title: Swedish man attacked by transgender woman in Pattaya
items.desc: Swedish man attacked by transgender woman in Pattaya Legacy A 59 year old Swedish man was injured after being assaulted by a transgender woman in Jomtien, Pattaya , Chon Buri , in the early hours of Saturday, March 22. At about 2.49am, the Sawang Boriboon Thammasathan Pattaya Rescue Centre received an emergency call for assistance at a residence in Wat Thammasamakkhi Soi 3. Volunteers arrived to find Magnus waiting outside his accommodation with visible swelling on his head and cuts on both knees. He received first aid on the scene before being transported to Pattaya Memorial Hospital for furt

items.title: Thailand to cut visa-free stay to 30 days, Foreign Ministry says
items.desc: Thailand to cut visa-free stay to 30 days, Foreign Ministry says Legacy Thailand’s Foreign Ministry is preparing to ask the government to cut the visa-free stay for eligible foreign tourists from 60 days to 30 days , Foreign Minister Sihasak Phuangketkaeo said on March 20. Sihasak said the 60-day visa exemption policy was introduced under the government of Srettha Thavisin to support Thai tourism, which he noted remains important to the country’s economic growth. However, he said the committee that considers visa policy, chaired by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, reviewed the measure and conc

items.title: 泰国商业部召集9大零售巨头，商讨物价管控
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月21日，泰国国内贸易厅厅长威他功在与9家大型消费品企业会谈后表示，为评估中东冲突对原油相关原料和货运的影响，国内贸易厅与联合利华、协成昌、宝洁、NEO、Osotspa、BJC、F&N、雀巢和Dutch Mill就塑料包装和塑料瓶等产品运输延误可能导致的供应紧张进行了商讨。9家企业确认，目前均库存充足，至少4月底前可维持现在的价格，但如果冲突持续，未来的商品价格难免受到波及。 威他功进一步表示，商业部正积极协调相关部门，设法缓解生产成本压力，尤其是塑料粒子、硫和溶剂等原料供应瓶颈，同时简化手续和限制。另外，为了确保供应能撑到5月，相关部门已准备与韩国进行谈判，争取放宽溶剂出口限制，并尽快寻找其他备用进口来源。 国内贸易厅还计划依据1999版《商品和服务价格管理法》升级法律措施，将6类必需品从“事后申报价格变动”改为“每次调涨前必须申请许可”，以减轻民众生活压力。这6类商品包括：卫生纸及面巾纸、洗发水、洗衣粉及洗衣液、洗洁精、卫生巾和肥皂。预计下周将召开听证会，之后征求政府部门的意见，再提交由商业部部长担任主席的中央商品和服务价格委员会审议。 在稽查方面，国内贸易厅已要求严格按照计量法对液化气灌装厂和零售店的充装量进行检查，与此同时也考虑修订仓库、筒仓、冷库得相关法规，把企业自有仓库纳入监管范围。此后，运营商必须申报仓库内得存货信息，以便检查人员随时查验，

items.title: Thailand plans to cut visa-free stay for foreigners from 60 to 30 days
items.desc: BANGKOK — Thailand’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs is preparing to propose reducing the visa-free stay period for foreign tourists from 60 days to 30 days, citing concerns over misuse of the scheme. Foreign Minister Sihasak Phuangketkeow said the current 60-day visa exemption, introduced under the previous government to boost tourism, may be longer than necessary. He noted that 30 days should be sufficient for most visitors, while tourists would still be able to apply for a 30-day extension if needed. He added that the proposed measure is not targeted at any specific nationality, but aims to add

items.title: 警惕脑膜炎！泰国今年已有3例死亡
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月22日，泰国总理府办公室副发言人艾琳透露，政府已指示公共卫生部疾病控制厅密切关注英国侵入性脑膜炎双球菌感染疫情的发展。根据英国卫生安全局（UKHSA）和欧洲疾病预防控制中心（ECDC）等国际卫生机构的数据，英国南部地区的疫情主要集中在学生群体，截至3月18日，累计报告约20例病例，其中9例确诊，至少2人死亡。泰国国内从今年1月1日至3月17日，共发现5例病例，其中3人死亡，但目前尚未发现这些病例与国外疫情有直接关联。 虽然病例数量不多，但疾控厅保持高度警惕，因为脑膜炎双球菌感染是一种法定须报告的严重传染病。一旦发现疑似病例，必须立即上报，哪怕只确认1例，也会迅速开展流行病学调查，尽快控制传播。脑膜炎双球菌感染的典型症状是突然高烧、剧烈头痛、颈部僵硬、意识模糊，有些人的皮肤会出现出血点或瘀斑，如果发现这些症状，一定要尽快就医，并且主动告知医生近期旅行史或接触史，如是否去过疫情报告地区，或者是否接触过疑似或确诊患者。 然而，脑膜炎双球菌感染可以用抗生素治疗，而且可以接种预防疫苗，计划前往高风险国家和地区的人，应提前至少10天向卫生服务单位咨询疫苗接种相关事宜，尤其是MenB血清型疫苗。 由于脑膜炎双球菌主要通过飞沫和共用物品传播，政府建议民众做好个人防护，勤洗手，尽量不要用手碰脸，在人群密集处佩戴口罩，避免与有发烧、咳嗽、打喷嚏等呼吸道症状的人近距离接触，儿童、青

items.title: Bangkok arrests 16 foreign women over alleged prostitution
items.desc: Bangkok arrests 16 foreign women over alleged prostitution Legacy A late-night prostitution crackdown in Bangkok led to 16 arrests on March 20 after complaints on social media alleged a group of foreign women were approaching visitors to offer sexual services on Sukhumvit Soi 4. The joint operation took place at about 8.30 pm in Khlong Toei district. Officers from Lumpini Police Station worked alongside officials from the Department of Women’s Affairs and Family Development under the Ministry of Social Development and Human Security, as well as Immigration Bureau Division 1. Officers conducted

items.title: Which country is happiest in Southeast Asia in 2026?
items.desc: BANGKOK — Finland has been ranked the world’s happiest country for the ninth consecutive year in the latest World Happiness Report 2026 , with Nordic nations continuing to dominate the global top 10. The annual rankings, compiled by Gallup , the Oxford Wellbeing Research Centre and the UN Sustainable Development Solutions Network , are based on a three-year average of how residents in 140 countries evaluate their own lives. The report also considers factors such as GDP per capita, social support, life expectancy, perceived freedom, generosity and levels of corruption. In Southeast Asia, Singap

items.title: 泰国严打旅游房地产“代持”，4家旅行社被吊销执照
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月21日，泰国商业部商业发展厅厅长普蓬透露，3月18日至20日期间，商业发展厅“人头公司”打击小组、旅游厅东部地区旅游业务和导游登记办公室、旅游警察、特别案件调查厅（DSI）、移民局和春武里府商业办等，在春武里府芭提雅市展开联合行动，对旅游及相关服务业、房地产业的疑似“人头公司”进行清查。 此次行动从4家会计师事务所和律师事务所入手，其在同一地址注册了多家公司，且其中一名泰国籍股东个人名下持有超过100家公司的股份，总投资额不低于3亿泰铢。该情形与“人头公司”高度相似，即泰国人代外国人持股，或提供协助、咨询，帮助外国人从事1999版《外国人经营法》禁止或需特别许可的业务。相关部门已要求涉事人员立即说明情况，若确认违法，将依法严办到底。 随后，联合行动小组对旅游公司进行了实地检查。经初步判断，有4家企业在更改董事后已不符合2008版《旅游业及导游法》第17条第1款的规定，行动小组当场在其营业场所外张贴吊销营业执照的公告。这4家公司分别为： – ALETIA TOURS CO.,LTD（许可证号 12/03325） – YOR INDO-THAI GROUP COMPANY LIMITED（许可证号 14/00404） – YHJH CO., LTD（许可证号 14/04490） – DI V-EXT CO., LTD

items.title: Thailand oil crisis poll shows unease and doubts over reserves
items.desc: Thailand oil crisis poll shows unease and doubts over reserves Legacy A new NIDA Poll released today, March 22, found that many adults in Thailand are worried about the country’s oil crisis , with more than half reporting daily difficulties, and a large share expressing little confidence in the government’s ability to maintain a stable oil supply . The survey, titled “Oil shortage: Talk to your partner, Switch off the lights”, was conducted by the NIDA Poll centre at the National Institute of Development Administration between March 17 and 18. It reportedly questioned 1,310 people aged 18 and

items.title: Samut Sakhon fishing crisis: fuel price hike forces boats to dock
items.desc: Samut Sakhon fishing crisis: fuel price hike forces boats to dock Legacy Samut Sakhon fishermen face dockings as ‘green oil’ price rises 6.5 baht per litre The Samut Sakhon fishing industry is under mounting financial pressure as the price of ‘green oil’ rises by 6.5 baht per litre on March 22, forcing many fishermen to dock their boats and prompting growing calls for government intervention. Mongkhon Mongkhontrilak, chairman of the Samut Sakhon Fisheries Association, said the price will rise from 34.3 baht to 40.8 baht per litre for cash purchases, with credit purchase

items.title: 泰国计划开发邮轮码头，助力建设海上旅游枢纽
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月21日，泰国交通部与皇家加勒比集团（RCG）举行会谈，共同讨论在泰国湾和安达曼海建设邮轮码头的规划，旨在解决现有设施拥挤的问题，同时提升接待大型邮轮的能力，带动国家经济，推动泰国成为区域海上旅游中心。本次会谈由交通部次长潘亚主持，皇家加勒比集团亚洲区公共关系部副总监Wendy Yamazaki和泰国港务局（PAT）的代表参会。 潘亚表示，泰国政府重视全球邮轮旅游快速增长的趋势，正全力开发大型邮轮码头，并通过公私合营模式，吸引民营企业参与建设与运营。目前已锁定苏梅岛、普吉岛和泰国湾上部等区域，同时正在评估芭提雅和梭桃邑之间的最佳位置，以扩大全球旅游网络至泰国东西两岸海域。 作为全球领先的邮轮公司，RCG拥有69只船队，航线遍布世界各地。今年RCG计划安排超45个邮轮航次停靠泰国港口，预计将吸引约12万名游客入境。然而，海上旅游的扩张也带来了相应挑战，双方就解决缺乏符合国际标准的大型邮轮码头这一问题坦诚交换了意见。为了让投资方提前做好准备，交通部正在整理林查班港的发展规划，以及其他高潜力区域的邮轮码头项目阶段计划表，准备提交给RCG和其他潜在投资者，供他们参考及进行后续的商业布局。 （编译：KA；来源：posttoday） The post 泰国计划开发邮轮码头，助力建设海上旅游枢纽 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

items.title: 1.3 million meth pills worth 39 million baht found by Mekong
items.desc: 1.3 million meth pills worth 39 million baht found by Mekong Legacy A village security team patrolling the banks of the Mekong River in Nong Khai province discovered three large packages containing around 1.3 million methamphetamine pills worth over 39 million baht on March 20. Police believe the drugs were part of a trafficking network’s attempt to move narcotics deeper into the country. The find was announced at a press conference at the Sangkhom district office on March 21. Nong Khai governor Saransak Srikruanet, provincial police commander Atthachon Chuangkan, Sangkhom district chief

items.title: Department of Disease Control targets online nicotine pouch sellers
items.desc: Department of Disease Control targets online nicotine pouch sellers Legacy Thailand’s Department of Disease Control (DDC) has lodged a complaint over the alleged online sale of nicotine pouches , after monitoring found the products were being advertised and sold digitally, with further checks pointing to a physical shop in Pathum Thani . Speaking yesterday, March 21, deputy director-general of the DDC Anek Moong-om-klang said that on March 17, he assigned investigators at Mueang Nonthaburi Police Station to pursue legal action against those accused of selling nicotine pouches online. The DDC s

items.title: 泰国寺庙面临“油缺”，遗体或无法及时火化
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月21日，泰国北榄府多个地区的寺庙正面临柴油严重短缺的问题，而柴油是火化仪式不可或缺的燃料，进而导致宗教仪式无法正常进行。据悉，部分寺庙已开始考虑改用传统柴火焚烧，或先将遗体冷藏保存暂延火化，直到柴油供应恢复。 火葬管理人员透露，若改用柴火，可能会产生大量烟尘，影响周边社区与环境，而且现在的火化炉多为现代化设计，已不适用柴火，需重新评估操作方式。目前，他们已四处求购柴油，但多数加油站点都贴出了“柴油售罄”告示，无法正常采购，民众火化仪式因此持续受阻。 一名火葬管理人员表示，今天他跑遍各站，都没有柴油可买，即使少数有售的加油站点也限购1000泰铢，根本不够一具遗体使用。通常火化每具遗体平均需要40–60升柴油，因体型差异而异，一次一般需准备80升左右，以防不足而延误时间。他担心如果明天有火化需求，将无柴油可用，后续若完全买不到柴油，寺庙只能考虑临时改用木柴或其他可燃物，但现代火化炉难以适应，燃烧不完全会产生大量黑烟，她坦言非常头疼。 Phra Pradaeng县Bang Phung镇Wat Paichayapol Ratchaworawiharn的代理住持说，该寺庙的火化仪式暂时未受太大影响，因为还能买到油。但如果后续缺油，只能与施主商量先冷藏遗体等待，或由施主自行去找柴油。 加油站女员工小萍认为，这段时间确实是“柴油危机”。油罐车虽照常送油，但供货量远远不足客户

items.title: 6人被捕，泰警捣毁一中资电诈窝点
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月20日，泰国第一警区、暖武里府地方警察局、Pakkred警察局联合宣布，成功捣毁一中资电信诈骗窝点，缴获总值超1400万泰铢的涉案物资，并逮捕6名嫌疑人，包括31岁的中国籍男性主犯WANG QINSEN、46岁的泰国籍男性华人XUCHEN XU、60岁的泰国籍女子塔提（音译）、39岁中国籍男子ZHENQIU CHEN、43岁中国籍男子SHIXIONG XU和51岁中国籍女子CHUOING ZHOU。 本次行动源于去年9月6日一位退休女公务员受害者的报警，她表示接到了自称“某医院财务人员”的电话，对方说她没提交办理养老金账户保护手续的文件，无法查看养老金单据，并要求她尽快提交身份证和户籍证明，以保护全部5个账户。当受害者说她不方便办理时，对方改口说要先申请OTP验证码。 一周后，又有自称“中央审计署官员”的人再次致电，声称需要受害者提供6位验证码办理“个人数据保护”（PDPA），以保障存款账户安全、养老金记录和下载薪资单据。在受害者同意后，对方添加她为Line好友并向其发送列有受害者个人信息及各银行账户名称的“中央审计署”文件。随后，对方指示受害者打开银行App，诱导她在账号栏填入文件中的号码、在金额栏填入分行代码，导致她不知不觉完成转账。受害者前后共转账两次，总计762,995泰铢，在无法联系对方时才发现自己受骗，于是向Pakkred警察局报案。 经进一步调查

items.title: “阿努廷2.0”或下周正式就职，4月7-9日发布施政方针
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月22日，泰国总理阿努廷预计将于3月24日之前将“阿努廷2.0内阁”名单呈请国王御准，如获批准，新内阁有望在下周晚些时候正式就职，看守内阁随之结束任期。另外，泰自豪党已确定于4月7-9日在国会发布政府施政方针。 有消息称，泰自豪党作为组阁核心政党，目前正与为泰党商讨新政府的施政方针。新的方针将延续泰自豪党的政策基调，强调“快速、精准、见效”，并在经济刺激等关键领域加上“Plus”政策，合称“泰国十项Plus”（Thailand 10 Plus），包含四个方向：普惠增长政策、提升竞争力政策、经济刺激与债务管理政策、创造收入的产业政策。 同时还将推动多项配套法案，包括清理2026财年剩余预算的《预算转移法》草案、将“旅游”移交文化部的《部门转移法》草案、《民众许可审批与服务便利法》草案，以及《家乡安居法》草案。然而，由于《石油基金补偿借款紧急法令》是看守政府时期已执行的措施，此次未被纳入到即将向国会报告的施政方针框架中。 为泰党则基于其47项政策提出解决民生、强化社区、推动教育、科技与创新的相关法案，重点包括《教育改革法》草案和《人力基金法》草案的能，将一并提交国会审议。 （编译：KA；来源：thaipbs） The post “阿努廷2.0”或下周正式就职，4月7-9日发布施政方针 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

items.title: 泰国打击跨国废物走私获联合国点赞
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月22日，联合国毒品和犯罪问题办公室（UNODC）于近日发文称，泰国3月10日在林查班港成功查获了一批非法进口电子废物，体现出泰国保护环境和民众健康的强大执法机制和多部门协作，也证明了泰国在打击跨国非法废物运输方面的领导作用。泰国政府高层亲自到场检查集装箱是一个明确的政策信号，表明泰国坚决打击跨国废物走私问题的决心，这一犯罪行为复杂且影响广泛，对合法循环经济体系的稳定性具有破坏性。 UNODC认为，林查班港查获行动的成功源于各部门系统性配合：特别案件调查厅（DSI）负责情报收集与犯罪资金追踪；海关厅作为第一道防线，及时发现运输异常并扣押货物；污染控制厅提供技术支持，对货物进行有害物质检测和环境影响评估，并向《巴塞尔公约》秘书处报告违规情况，以启动废物退运程序。 跨国废物走私是全球面临的新兴威胁之一，这种非法生意利润丰厚，且在一些国家犯罪风险较低，导致危险废物不断转移到往往缺乏安全处置能力的发展中国家。该问题不仅威胁公众健康与生态系统，还削弱了合法回收产业作为长期循环经济核心支柱的作用。 在UNODC的WasteNet项目框架的指导下，泰国工厂管理厅、国家警察总署等多部门密切合作，通过制定标准作业程序、技术培训、情报共享及跨境协调等，提升泰国侦测、预防与应对非法废物流动的能力。这与泰国“生物-循环-绿色经济”战略高度契合，同时也切实履行了国际有害废物跨境转移控制义
